task: Put the blue block on the orange plate
raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A blue block is on a wooden table. An orange plate is in the sink. The blue block is a small, rigid cube; the orange plate is a large, flat dish. STRATEGY - First, reach for and grasp the blue block. Then, transport it to the orange plate. Finally, place the block on the plate. Failure modes include dropping the block during transport or misplacing it on the table. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the block at its body for initial contact. Step 2 grasp the block at its body for secure hold. Step 3 transport the block to the plate's surface. Step 4 place the block on the plate's surface.
Goal: on(blue_block, orange_plate)
Scene: blue_block, orange_plate, table
Step 1: reach(blue_block)
  affordance_region: the body of the blue block
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, blue_block, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, blue_block) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(blue_block)
  affordance_region: the body of the blue block
  contact: holding(blue_block) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(blue_block)
Step 3: transport(blue_block -> orange_plate)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the orange plate
  spatial: above(blue_block, orange_plate, 0.05) [completion]
  safety: no_drop(blue_block)
  done_when: above(blue_block, orange_plate, 0.05)
Step 4: place(blue_block -> orange_plate)
  affordance_region: an empty area on the orange plate
  contact: surface_contact(blue_block, orange_plate) [completion]; released(blue_block) [completion]
  pose: stable(blue_block) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(blue_block, orange_plate) AND released(blue_block)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the blue block
the body of the blue block
an empty area on the orange plate
an empty area on the orange plate
